title: StarCar train manifest (intent-record payload)
description: Spec 2026-07-23-yard-board S2: the manifest contract LAYERED over starcar-artifact/1 (Law 6 - this file does not restate the base record contract; validators run BOTH schemas against a record). A manifest is an intent-kind record whose subject carries the mandatory train: prefix (design rev 5 DR3-3 partition rule, Q7 ruling: the prefix is a disjointness guarantee, and the train id is the WHOLE subject string - no consumer ever strips the prefix for meaning). Membership and roles are conductor-DECLARED originals (D16); status never flows from a manifest.

subject.description: For manifests only: the train identity, whole-string. The train: prefix partitions the shared subject namespace away from producer-minted dispatch ids (design DR3-3; vector fold/subject-partition.json).

manifest.description: The single new top-level key (design DR3-1 item 4: ONE key added to the wire schema and every typed decoder's known key-set, so D17's unknown-field disclosure never fires on a well-formed manifest).

tickets.description: Issue refs establishing provenance (ticket -> approved train -> manifest).

members.description: Declared consist, in order. Membership only - liveness/outcome stay the fold's (D16).

role.description: Recognition vocabulary lives in schema/vocab/roles.json as DATA (Law 7). Unrecognised roles are detector discoveries, never validation failures.

gate.description: Present when role is gate: names WHICH gate (e.g. design-review round 3). Split from the role token so recognition vocab stays unparameterised (spec S2 deviation note - supersedes the design's illustrative gate:name single token).

allOf.$comment: SB-1 (spec review round 1, folded): formerly a negative-lookahead pattern, which .NET accepts but Go's RE2 regexp REJECTS at schema-load time (proven with this repo's toolchain) - a schema only one language can load breaks the one-schema-both-languages thesis (Law 6, D15). This lookahead-free form expresses the same rule - a record whose subject does NOT start with train: (or has none) must not carry a manifest - using only plain anchored patterns, RE2-safe.
